items.title: Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving while disqualified

children.content: Former professional cyclist and two-time Olympian Rohan Dennis pleads guilty after he was caught driving while disqualified, not far from where his wife died two-and-a-half years ago.

gallery.altText: Former Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis outside the Adelaide Magistrates Court.

thumbnail.altText: Former Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis outside the Adelaide Magistrates Court.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Federal MP and staffer injured in boating accident

children.content: Tasmanian police say a man fell from an oyster barge when it hit a sandbar and was briefly trapped. Member for Barker Tony Pasin says he and a staffer were injured and now receiving medical treatment, but it is not clear who fell from the barge.

image.altText: A man with a Liberal logo on his jacket standing in front of a garden

items.title: Alleged 'fight club' organiser arrested, SA Police say

children.content: SA Police have arrested the alleged organiser of a 'fight club' and reported seven other teenagers they allege were linked to videos of amateur bouts that were posted online.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: ATO asked to justify 'extravagant' $73m Adelaide office move

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office will spend at least $73.4 million to relocate its offices into "the best building in Adelaide", four streets away.

gallery.altText: Two high-rise office building in a green open space

thumbnail.altText: Two high-rise office building in a green open space

items.title: SA victims of alleged childcare centre abuser identified, AFP says

children.content: "A couple" of alleged victims from South Australia have been identified as part of an investigation into a Sydney childcare worker accused of abusing more than 130 children, federal police say.

image.altText: Cloise up of man grinning in a green shirt, plant behind him

items.title: Ambulances arriving late to lower-priority patients, SA opposition says

children.content: Data obtained by the South Australian opposition shows fewer ambulances are arriving on time to priority 3 and 4 patients.

image.altText: An ambulance driving on a city street with buildings on either side

items.title: Controversial 'blue blob' makes it to first birthday relatively unscathed

children.content: The blue sculpture that's been the subject of love, loathing, debate and international headlines for googly eyed-vandalism has made it to its first birthday relatively unscathed.

gallery.altText: A blue public art statue wearing a large party hat

thumbnail.altText: A blue public art statue wearing a large party hat

items.title: Community meets over plans for large 'AI factory' at River Murray town

children.content: An AI company says its proposed projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs in South Australia, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

image.altText: Tailem Bend locals at a public forum.

items.title: Hopes bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory

children.content: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

image.altText: An animal bone at the bottom of sinkhole with a diver in a black wetsuit and holding a torch.

items.title: Researchers develop laser technology to test drinks for methanol

children.content: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.

image.altText: A man with glasses wearing a grey jumper poses next to a glass bottle with brown fluid in it

items.title: Australia's wine grape crush plummets to lowest in more than 25 years

children.content: Australian wine grape production has fallen to its lowest amount in more than 25 years as the industry adjusts to a combination of depressed market conditions.

image.altText: A farmer in green shirt leans on the end of a winegrape vineyard holding his hat

items.title: Parklands chop won't stop despite petition triggering parliamentary inquiry

children.content: Acting Premier Kyam Maher says works for the golf course redevelopment and MotoGP will continue despite a petition attracting 13,500 signatures.

gallery.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.

thumbnail.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.

items.title: Coronial inquest recommends SA Police improve suicide training

children.content: The coronial inquest into the death of William "Bill" Stockman did not confirm whether his death was preventable, but made multiple recommendations to the SA police minister.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a blue shirt sitting in front of a white background

items.title: Death of man experiencing homelessness in rural town prompts call for help

children.content: Millicent residents say they are concerned for the welfare of dozens of people sleeping rough in town.

image.altText: A man in a grey jumper in a building with graffiti on the wall

images.altText: Senator Sarah Hanson-Young sits at a panel with two other people.

articles.title: Telstra outage caused by system being sent back two decades

children.content: The national Telstra outage last week was triggered by maintenance work that caused the system's clocks to go back two decades, the telco says.

image.altText: The orange and white telstra sign is in focus in the background as people walk past in a blur.

articles.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

image.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

children.content: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady is appearing before a Senate hearing over the over the nationwide outage, and potential compensation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Senator Hanson-Young calls Telstra 'smug' during outage hearing

updates.title: Telstra has paid out $100,000 in credit for compensation so far
Telstra not able to produce total outage costs today

items.title: Hanson laments end of White Australia policy on Tommy Robinson podcast

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has blamed the end of the White Australia policy for migration issues in the country during a controversial podcast appearance with a far-right activist in the UK.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks in the Senate.

items.title: A gulf has opened up between the Liberals and the zeitgeist

children.content: The mismatch between where the public resides and what the Liberals are expounding is a deeper challenge — a gulf perhaps unable to be bridged, at least in the short term.

image.altText: A man in a dark suit stares glibly toward the camera

items.title: UK urges FIFA to act after Argentine players hold up Malvinas banner

children.content: The British government urges FIFA to investigate after Argentine players held up a banner declaring the Falkland Islands were Argentinian.

articles.title: When is the World Cup final? All you need to know about Spain vs Argentina

children.content: The World Cup final is nearly upon us, with Spain to face Argentina for the ultimate prize. Here's everything you need to know about the biggest game in football.

image.altText: Argentina and Spain badges either side of a 2026 World Cup logo

items.title: 'Four billionaires and the Chinese government': Unions to take AI fight to Labor conference

children.content: Labor insiders expect ongoing debate about artificial intelligence when party faithful gather to hash out a policy platform to guide the next three years.

image.altText: Sally McManus standing in front of an ACTU banner.

items.title: Sam Neill died from a common infection. Here's how it turned deadly

children.content: After the actor's death from pneumonia, here's what to know about the common infection, its symptoms and why it can sometimes become deadly.

image.altText: A close up of Sam Neill with tears in his eyes

items.title: 'Carry on patriots': Trump officials defend pilot after fighter jet stunt

children.content: Officials in US President Donald Trump's administration, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, continue to support low-altitude military flyovers despite growing safety concerns after a jet was filmed buzzing a crowded beach.

articles.title: ICE agent involved in fatal shooting violent and unstable, family says

children.content: Close relatives of the ICE agent understood to have killed a Colombian man in the US this week say he has struggled with mental health issues for most of his life.

image.altText: Blood is seen on the pavement near the scene of a shooting involving ICE.

articles.title: Smoke from Canada wildfires crosses into US prompting warnings to stay indoors

children.content: Heavy, pungent wildfire smoke darkens skies in the US from the Great Lakes to parts of the East Coast, reducing visibility and prompting warnings that breathing the air outside could be dangerous.

image.altText: A pedestrian crosses a street, as the high-rise skyline in the background is obscured in a grey haze.

items.title: Australia's number one song now has AI credits on Spotify

children.content: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.

image.altText: Josh Fawaz standing in a club setting throwing up two peace signs

items.title: Will Ferrell channels Ricky Bobby in TV return

children.content: Plus a noir mystery set in outback Coober Pedy, a true crime drama which doesn't exploit its victims, and more new TV to watch this month.

image.altText: A man in a visor exits a bus

items.title: 'People want something different': Ardern on the rise of fringe politics

children.content: The former New Zealand prime minister reflects on leading through crisis, becoming a mother while running a country and why she believes voters are craving something different.

items.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry

children.content: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

image.altText: An uber sign on a car.

items.title: Vaping in decline as young Australians shift to other nicotine products

children.content: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

image.altText: A hand holding a purple vape, Bunbury tower in the background

items.title: Community rallies together after Jana Armstrong's alleged murder

children.content: Darling Downs bands together after the third woman allegedly murdered by a man known to her in 18 months.

gallery.altText: colourful flowers on the fence of a house

thumbnail.altText: colourful flowers on the fence of a house

items.title: 'Substantial clean-up effort' needed for shuttered manganese smelter

children.content: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

image.altText: A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.

items.title: 'Best place to look for life': Atmosphere discovered on Earth-like planet

children.content: The search for life beyond Earth has marked a new discovery — evidence of an atmosphere on an Earth-like planet beyond our Solar System with modelling suggesting it may be a water world.

image.altText: Artists impression of LHS 1140 b orbiting its star.

items.title: Protesters criticise Zelenskyy after Ukrainian defence minister's sacking

children.content: A dispute between the now former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov and Kyiv's top general is spilling into public, after Volodymyr Zelenskyy orders a second reshuffle of this year.

gallery.altText: A person holds a Ukrainian flag as two other protesters hold signs in Ukrainian in a public square.

thumbnail.altText: A person holds a Ukrainian flag as two other protesters hold signs in Ukrainian in a public square.

items.title: Father 'disgusted' over inadequacy of charges after Laos methanol poisoning

children.content: A Melbourne father whose daughter died from methanol poisoning in Laos has said he is "disgusted" over the inadequacy of charges expected to be laid.

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: Win for MS patients facing drug pricing cliff

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler has accepted independent advice for a rapid review of MS drugs to ensure they remain available on the PBS.

image.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: One of leading causes of infant deaths sees 'fantastic' decline in NSW

children.content: New South Wales health authorities have hailed a vaccination program that has nearly halved hospitalisations for a potentially deadly respiratory virus among newborn babies a "fantastic" success, as the winter illness peak fast approaches.

image.altText: A close up of a woman in beige knitwear holding a baby, their faces are out of frame

items.title: AI needs more memory. South Korea is selling it at a price

children.content: South Korea is doubling down on its bid to lead in AI, unveiling plans last month to spend hundreds of billions on developing its chip manufacturing.

image.altText: A man takes a photo using his smartphone of an electronic advertising board with the SK hynix logo.

items.title: Decade-long friendship between Roy Orbison's hit maker and Australian singer

children.content: Every Monday morning for the last 14 years, Australian musician Damien Leith has been meeting with the late Joe Melson, the Nashville songwriter responsible for some of Roy Orbison's biggest hits.

gallery.altText: Damien plays a guitar while sitting in a back car seat next to Joe Melson, who wears a black coat.
